description: Figure 12 presents Mott-Schottky analysis and carrier density data for low-density steels (LDSs) in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution. Panel (a) plots C^-2 versus applied potential (E vs. SCE) for the as-hot-rolled sample S0 and solution-treated samples S950, S1050, and S1150, with a dashed line separating Region 1 and Region 2. Caption reports that Region 1 (-1.13 to -0.93 V_SCE) displays positive slopes characteristic of n-type semiconductors, whereas Region 2 (-0.93 to -0.67 V_SCE) exhibits negative slopes indicating p-type behavior. The S950 curve reaches the highest C^-2 peak in the n-type region, while S0 shows the lowest. Panel (b) reports the calculated donor density (N_D) and acceptor density (N_A). S0 registers the highest defect concentrations (N_D = 0.70 x 10^21 cm^-3, N_A = 2.25 x 10^21 cm^-3), whereas S950 records the lowest (N_D = 0.26 x 10^21 cm^-3, N_A = 0.68 x 10^21 cm^-3). As the solution-treatment temperature rises from 950 to 1150 °C, both N_D and N_A increase progressively yet remain below the S0 level. These findings indicate that the finest grain structure (S950) minimizes oxide-film defect densities, thereby reducing penetration channels for chloride ions and improving corrosion resistance relative to coarser-grained conditions.
